## Summary

This May 2026 comparison (published May 18, 2026 - among the most recent sources in the research set) directly addresses the Ahrefs vs. Semrush decision for the stinger's exact target audience. The guide notes Semrush's October 2025 "Semrush One" launch: a bundle that adds AI visibility tracking (monitoring how your brand shows up in ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews) to the core SEO toolkit. This is a significant addition for the 2026 context because it means Semrush now covers both traditional keyword research and AI citation monitoring in a single platform.

The comparison is clear-eyed: Ahrefs is better for backlink research and has a cleaner interface; Semrush is better for content strategy and keyword research depth, has a free trial, and costs slightly less at entry level. For indie hackers specifically, the recommendation is Semrush Pro for most cases, with Ahrefs only preferred if backlinks are the primary growth lever (unlikely for an early-stage content strategy).

The pricing data is precise and current (as of May 2026): Ahrefs Starter at $29/month (new January 2026), Ahrefs Lite at $129/month; Semrush Pro at $139.95/month ($117.33 annual). Semrush One (SEO + AI visibility) starts at ~$165/month annual. The "Semrush One" addition is important for founders who want to track both SEO and AI citation performance without juggling multiple tools.

## Key data points / quotes

- Semrush launched "Semrush One" in October 2025: SEO toolkit + AI visibility tracker (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews).
- Ahrefs January 2026 change: launched $29/month Starter plan (first major pricing change).
- Semrush Pro: $139.95/month ($117.33 annual) - 5 projects, 500 keywords, full Keyword Magic Tool.
- Ahrefs Lite: $129/month (~$108 annual) - best tier for indie hackers wanting proper backlink data.
- Semrush One (SEO + AI visibility bundle): starts at ~$165/month annual.
- "For most indie hackers under $5K MRR, Semrush Pro at $117/month annual is the better starting point."
- Decision condition for Ahrefs: "If backlinks are your primary growth lever."
- Ahrefs Webmaster Tools: free access to limited Site Explorer and Site Audit for verified site owners.
- Semrush Keyword Magic Tool: 26 billion keywords.

## Implications for stinger-forge

- The Semrush One bundle (SEO + AI visibility) is a significant 2026 development that should be mentioned in `guides/03-keyword-research-scoping.md` as an option for founders who want unified SEO + AEO tracking.
- The Ahrefs Webmaster Tools (free for verified site owners) is an often-overlooked free resource that should be listed alongside GSC in the free tool stack.
- The "backlinks as primary growth lever" decision condition for choosing Ahrefs helps clarify when the more expensive or less user-friendly tool is worth it.
- The recommendation structure (Semrush Pro for most, Ahrefs if backlink-focused, SE Ranking for budget, Mangools for keyword-only) should be the decision tree in `guides/03-keyword-research-scoping.md`.
